summary: Systems architecture review accepts the M5 dashboard/opportunity-list implementation. The slice keeps Prisma-backed query logic in lib/opportunities.ts, renders /dashboard and /opportunities as server components, returns serializable view models, uses deterministic score ordering and bounded queries, and does not introduce API, schema, package, global-style, scan-orchestration, or opportunity-detail creep.

notes: Product changes are confined to allowed task paths: app/dashboard/**, app/opportunities/**, components/**, lib/opportunities.ts, tests/**, and README.md.

Observed project/** changes are task-management artifacts: the task packet status/timestamp moved to REVIEW and the implementation handoff exists under project/messages/outbox. I did not treat these as product implementation creep.
No changes were found in app/api/**, prisma/**, package files, services/**, agents/**, lib/scoring.ts, lib/db.ts, lib/env.ts, app/page.tsx, app/layout.tsx, or app/globals.css.

evidence: lib/opportunities.ts:187 queries dashboard data through Prisma with date-bounded Opportunity/SearchRun where clauses, deterministic orderBy, aggregate/count metrics, and bounded take limits.
lib/opportunities.ts:259 centralizes opportunity-list Prisma access, normalizes query filters, applies scalar filters to the Prisma where clause, and bounds result sets.
lib/opportunities.ts:394 serializes Opportunity rows into plain view models, including ISO date strings, detailHref, score, task/run labels, risk cue, and monetization cue.
lib/opportunities.ts:448 and lib/opportunities.ts:480 apply deterministic in-memory filtering for derived risk and normalize query-string aliases safely.
lib/opportunities.ts:630 provides deterministic score sorting with createdAt and id tie-breakers.
app/dashboard/page.tsx:6 and app/opportunities/page.tsx:10 mark the routes dynamic server-rendered pages; neither adds client-side data fetching.
app/dashboard/page.tsx:84 renders the Top 5 through the reusable OpportunityCard and app/dashboard/page.tsx:113 renders persisted recent SearchRun rows.

app/opportunities/page.tsx:21 calls the shared data helper from the server page and app/opportunities/page.tsx:105 renders query-string filters for radarTaskId, minScore, toolType, riskLevel, and status.

components/OpportunityCard.tsx:26 renders score, title, keyword, task, market/language, tool type, summary, monetization/risk/build/status cues, and a stable detail link without implementing the detail route.
components/ScoreBadge.tsx:18 clamps and tones scores locally without recalculating scoring semantics.
tests/opportunities.test.ts:18 covers Top 5 ordering, filters, serialization, empty states, ScoreBadge, and OpportunityCard rendering.
README.md:290 documents the dashboard/list slice, local data dependency, supported filter parameters, validation commands, and explicit non-goals.

tests_run.result: PASS: working tree inspection showed README.md and the task packet modified, product implementation files untracked under allowed paths, and the implementation handoff present under project/messages/outbox.
PASS: tracked diff showed README.md and project/tasks/active/M5-DASHBOARD-OPPORTUNITY-LIST-001.json; untracked product files were inspected directly.
PASS: 6 focused tests passed across 2 suites.

PASS: Next.js production build completed and emitted /dashboard and /opportunities as dynamic server-rendered routes. Nonfatal local Windows SWC native-package warnings were printed before fallback compilation succeeded.
PASS: no whitespace errors; Git printed existing line-ending conversion warnings for README.md and the task packet.
PASS: systems_architect review handoff validates against the JustGoal handoff schema.

decisions: ACCEPT: The implementation satisfies the M5 systems acceptance criteria for dashboard/list pages, shared data helper, score-sorted Top 5/list behavior, query-string filters, serializable view models, component reuse, focused tests, and README documentation.
ACCEPT: The Prisma query shape is bounded and deterministic. Dashboard metrics use persisted aggregate/count queries instead of relying only on the visible Top 5 query window.
ACCEPT: Risk filtering is derived from existing persisted rawAnalysis, riskSummary, and scoreBreakdown fields without adding a schema column or migration, which is appropriate for this scoped slice.

ACCEPT: Detail links are stable targets only; no /opportunities/[id] route, status mutation, API route, scan trigger, RunScanButton wiring, RecentRunStatus component, schema change, package change, or global style change was introduced.
risks: Risk-level filtering is applied after fetching a bounded score-sorted window because risk is not an indexed scalar column. This is acceptable for the local MVP slice, but a future large dataset may need a dedicated persisted risk field or a narrower query strategy.
Opportunity detail links intentionally 404 until the separate detail task lands. This matches the task packet's non-goal boundary.
The dashboard average-risk cue is computed from the visible opportunity window rather than a full-dataset indexed aggregate because risk is derived from JSON/text fields.
requested_action: Proceed with domain_designer and qa_reviewer acceptance; no systems-architecture changes are requested.
